title: Technical Application Specialist - Campania - Apprendistato
description:
### **The role**


The Application Specialist supports the sales process and maximizes customer value through **pre‑sales and post‑sales technical support and education**. This role is essential in building strong customer relationships, ensuring high satisfaction levels, and acting as a trusted **brand ambassador for Abbott**.


You will work closely with Sales Representatives, Sales Managers, Scientific Liaison and office‑based teams to drive commercial success and organic growth.

### **Customer scope**


You will support a broad range of customer segments, including:

* Public and private hospitals
* Centralized and core laboratories
* Emergency Departments and other hospital units

### **Key responsibilities**

**Pre‑sales to post‑sales customer activities**

* Partner with the sales team to execute pre‑sales activities, including identification of technical stakeholders, product demonstrations, user trials, and tailored connectivity solutions
* Develop, adapt and track educational materials; capture and communicate evaluation outcomes
* Plan and execute new customer implementations, including workflow adaptation, device installation, connectivity setup, user training and post‑installation follow‑up
* Provide technical support to customers, including troubleshooting, software updates, new connectivity solutions and additional training
* Assess when to manage issues directly or escalate to European Technical Support, ensuring follow‑up until full resolution

**Ongoing collaboration**

* Act as a link between the Commercial team and Informatics to stay aligned on connectivity solutions
* Work closely with office teams to ensure documentation and procedures are accurate, compliant and properly recorded in the CRM system
* Share technical knowledge with the sales team to strengthen customer value and competitive differentiation (tips, best practices, device functionality)
* Support Sales and Management on strategic priorities and key accounts, proactively contributing ideas to improve customer satisfaction
* Contribute to tender submissions and contract preparation for key customers
* Collaborate with Marketing on product demonstrations, congresses, internal meetings and training events
* Work with Scientific Liaison on key site studies and KOL relationship

* Bachelor’s degree in **Biology, Biochemistry, Microbiology, Pharmacy or Biomedical Engineering**

### **Experience \& skills**

* **5\+ years of experience** in the medical or diagnostics industry (In Vitro Diagnostics preferred)
* Experience with **IT connectivity** of diagnostic or medical devices to hospital **LIS/HIS** systems
* Strong customer‑focused mindset with proven technical and commercial capabilities
* Excellent communication, teamwork and relationship‑building skills
* Strong time management, prioritization and ability to work autonomously
* Solid understanding of diagnostic products, technical solutions and industry terminology, with the ability to quickly learn new concepts
* High proficiency with digital tools and applications, including connectivity software, **Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint)**, **Salesforce.com**, and web‑conferencing platforms (Teams, WebEx)

### **Languages**

* **Italian:** fluent
* **English:** fluent
company_description: Abbott employees do work that matters as part of a global community dedicated to helping people live better and healthier with our life-changing technologies.

title: Pharma Medical Affairs: Direzione Medica nel settore Farmacetuco
description:
Alma Laboris ha aperto i colloqui per i percorsi online di eccellenza nell'Industria Farmaceutica, offrendo agevolazioni di inserimento professionale per il progresso di carriera. L'obiettivo primario è formare e specializzare individui da presentare alle aziende partner del network.


**Il percorso formativo, denominato "Master Pharma Medical Affairs: Direzione Medica nel Farmaceutico," include approfondimenti su argomenti chiave come:**



* Il Ciclo di Vita del Prodotto Farmaceutico


* L'Evoluzione accelerata del Medical Affairs: Ruoli, Responsabilità e Competenze


* Generare Evidenze per Creare Valore in Medical Affairs: Qualità, autenticità e prova di ogni risultato


* + Ricerca Pre\-Clinica e Clinica: dove nascono le "Evidenze"
	+ Elementi di Metodologia e Statistica: le "Evidenze" in Medicina
	+ Progettazione e Realizzazione delle "Evidenze"
	+ Farmacovigilanza e Medical Affairs: Il Paziente al Centro!
	+ Affari Regolatori, Affari Legali e Medical Affairs: un Rapporto Indissolubile
	+ Farmacoeconomia e Medical Affairs: dare Valore Economico alle Evidenze Cliniche
Il programma offre anche un supporto individuale da parte di un tutor esperto del settore, un portale "Network" per candidarsi alle offerte di lavoro delle aziende partner e la promozione del profilo per interviste in diverse posizioni aperte.



I requisiti per partecipare includono coloro che attualmente ricoprono ruoli aziendali, professionisti specializzati, titolari e/o collaboratori di aziende, laureati del settore e giovani professionisti provenienti da altri percorsi di studio previa valutazione. Il tasso di successo del placement è del 90%.



Alma Laboris Business School, con oltre 25 anni di esperienza nel settore della formazione, offre percorsi pratici di alta formazione per una rapida applicazione nel mondo del lavoro. Grazie alla sua competenza e al team qualificato, rappresenta un punto di riferimento per le aziende che cercano di potenziare e/o ampliare il proprio business attraverso profili specializzati.


**Con la presentazione della candidatura, i partecipanti riceveranno informazioni dettagliate.**

title: Pricing & Reimbursement Sr Advisor
description:
Pricing \& Reimbursement Sr Advisor


**Category:** Market Access
**Location:**Roma, Lazio, IT  

Are you ready to shape how innovative therapies reach the patients who need them most in Italy? Join us as a Pricing \& Reimbursement Sr Advisor and play a pivotal role in securing market access for treatments that change lives across serious chronic diseases.

  


**Your new role**  

As Pricing \& Reimbursement Sr Advisor, you will be at the heart of our market access efforts in Italy, building strong relationships with key relevant stakeholders, while securing timely reimbursement for our portfolio. You will navigate the complexity of the Italian healthcare system and translate the value of our products into compelling submissions and negotiations with AIFA.



Day\-to\-day your tasks will include:


* Leading the end\-to\-end preparation, submission and negotiation of Pricing \& Reimbursement dossiers to AIFA, ensuring alignment with local regulations and corporate strategic goals
* Developing local pricing strategies and value propositions, including budget impact models, cost\-effectiveness analyses, HTA and HEOR tailored to the Italian healthcare system
* Building and maintaining relationships with key stakeholders to shape the external environment
* Partnering with Global and Regional teams to influence pricing assumptions and secure alignment on local launch strategies
* Communicating the unique attributes of our therapeutic area products to external multi\-stakeholders to defend and maximise the value proposition
* Collaborating cross\-functionally with Medical, Marketing, Regulatory and Market Access colleagues to integrate P\&R strategy across the product lifecycle
* Monitoring AIFA procedures, therapeutic registries, regional funding dynamics to anticipate risks and identify opportunities

  


**Your new department**  

As part of External Affairs Department, our commitment is to serve patients and build high\-value, sustainable partnerships across the healthcare ecosystem. As a highly integrated and strategic division, the External Affairs encompasses Pricing \& Reimbursement, Regional Market Access, Public Affairs, and External Communications. Given the dynamic and complex nature of our environment, cross\-functional collaboration is our cornerstone. Within External Affairs, you will join our affiliate in Rome, working closely with a dedicated team committed to ensuring that patients in Italy benefit from our innovative therapies as quickly as possible. You will contribute directly to shaping the access landscape and bringing transformative treatments to the Italian healthcare system.

  


**Your skills \& qualifications**  

To be successful in this position, you have a scientific background and a strong track record in Market Access in the Italian pharmaceutical environment. You combine technical expertise with a customer\-centric mindset and the ability to influence across stakeholders.


* A scientific background and a minimum of 5 years' experience in Market Access, HTA or HEOR with a clear focus on pricing and reimbursement
* Deep understanding of the Italian pharmaceutical regulatory framework, including AIFA procedures, therapeutic registries, regional funding dynamics
* Proven ability to design and execute integrated payer value propositions, pricing and HE analyses
* Customer\-centric mindset with excellent stakeholder management skills, both internally and with external key opinion leaders
* Highly performant with a strong focus on delivering great results, even in complex environments with few defined procedures
* Fluent in English and Italian, both written and spoken

  


**Working at Novo Nordisk**  

Every day we seek the solutions that defeat serious chronic diseases. To do this, we approach our work with determination, constant curiosity and a commitment to finding better ways forward. For over 100 years, this dedication has driven us to build a company focused on lasting change for long\-term health. One where diverse thinking, shared purpose and mutual respect come together to create extraordinary results. When you join us, you're not just starting a job – you're becoming part of a story that spans generations.

  


**What we offer**



At Novo Nordisk, you’ll join a global healthcare company with a unique culture and strong results. We offer continuous learning, career development, and benefits tailored to your life and career stage.


**Salary:**For this role, the Annual Base Salary ranges from 53,180\.00 to 82,430\.00 EUR, corresponding to the level of the position.



The placement within the salary range will be assessed during the recruitment process based on the candidate’s skills, competencies, knowledge, and relevant experience.


**Incentives and Benefits:**The salary package may include short\-term and/or long\-term incentives as well as other employee benefits based on position level, location, functional area and relevant market benchmarks.



Learn more about our Reward Philosophy here.

  


**Deadline**  

24 June 2026\. Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis, so please submit your application as soon as possible.


We commit to an inclusive recruitment process and equality of opportunity for all our job applicants.

At Novo Nordisk, we're not chasing quick fixes – we're creating lasting change for long\-term health. For over 100 years, we've been driven by a single purpose: to defeat serious chronic diseases and help millions of people live healthier lives. This dedication fuels our constant curiosity and inspires us to push the boundaries of what's possible in healthcare. We embrace diverse perspectives, seek out bold ideas, and build partnerships rooted in shared purpose. Together, we're making healthcare more accessible, treating and preventing diseases, and pioneering solutions that create change spanning generations. When you join us, you become part of something bigger – a legacy of impact that reaches far beyond today.
company_description: Novo Nordisk is a global healthcare company, driving change to defeat diabetes and other serious chronic diseases such as obesity, and rare blood and rare endocrine disease

description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**


As part of the Thermo Fisher Scientific team, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life every single day to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our global teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while helping to take science a step beyond by developing solutions for some of the world’s toughest challenges, like protecting the environment, making sure our food is safe or helping find cures for cancer.  

  

DESCRIPTION:  

Join our collaborative team as a Business Management Manager at Thermo Fisher Scientific, where you'll contribute to customer success and business growth. You'll lead a team of Business Managers while serving as the primary liaison between our organization and key clients, ensuring exceptional service delivery and strong partnerships. This position offers the opportunity to develop strategic business relationships, optimize operational efficiency, and contribute directly to our mission of enabling customers to make the world healthier, cleaner, and safer.  

  

REQUIREMENTS:  

* Advanced Degree plus 6 years of experience, or Bachelor's Degree plus 8 years of experience in pharmaceutical/chemical industry, preferably in CMO/CDMO environment
* Preferred Fields of Study: Science, Business Management, or related technical field
* Additional relevant technical certifications in pharmaceutical or project management disciplines valued
* People management experience leading cross\-functional teams
* Proficiency in contract negotiations and Master Supply Agreement (MSA) management
* Financial acumen for budgeting, forecasting, and pricing strategies
* Experience in client relationship management and strategic account development
* Project management capabilities with focus on technology transfer and commercial operations
* Demonstrated success in business development and revenue growth
* Analytical and problem\-solving skills
* Effective communication and presentation abilities
* Fluent in English; additional language skills may be considered
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and relevant ERP systems
* Knowledge of cGMP regulations and pharmaceutical industry standards
* Ability to travel up to 30% for client meetings and site visits
* Experience in managing multi\-million dollar client portfolios
* Change management and process improvement capabilities

title: Field Service Engineer - Medical in Tuscany
description:
### **Why join Stryker?**


Looking for a place that values your unique talents? Discover Stryker's award\-winning culture.  

We are proud to offer you our total rewards package which includes bonuses, healthcare, insurance benefits, retirement programs, wellness programs, as well as service and performance awards – not to mention various social and recreational activities, all of which are location specific.### **Job description**


We are excited to be named one of the World’s Best Workplaces by Fortune Magazine!
  

We are proud to offer you our total rewards package which includes bonuses, healthcare, insurance benefits, retirement programs, wellness programs, as well as service and performance awards – not to mention various social and recreational activities, all of which are location specific.  

As Field Service Engineer you will be responsible for the repair and preventative maintenance of Stryker products in the Medical division, ensuring compliance with the quality system and manufacturer specifications. You will provide technical support and documentation to internal and external customers.

**Please apply with your CV in English.**

**Geographical area**: Tuscany

**Responsibilities**

* Repair and inspect Stryker electronic/mechanical products (hospital beds and stretchers, ambulance stretchers, defibrillators).
* Perform annual inspection and calibration according to the MDR, at the customer's site.
* Use software and technology tools to properly document and archive technical reports of performed activities and access technical information.
* Assist the Quality Department/customer with any product recalls, field safety alerts, and field action notices.
* Fulfil service contracts with scheduled maintenance.
* Provide technical training to internal and external personnel.
* Perform installation of Stryker systems at the customer's site.
* Perform software and hardware updates.
* Provide technical support to internal (sales team) and external customers
* Efficient and comprehensive documentation of service activities for internal and external use
* Ensure billing for services provided and contribute to achieving sales goals
* Ensure the traceability of all Stryker products in the field through reporting tools.
* Ensure excellent follow\-up on all customer issues.
* Schedule customer visits in collaboration with the sales team or customer service

**Working Conditions**

* Work will take place in a variety of settings, including the shop floor, hospitals, and various customer sites.
* Field Service Engineers will interact with key customers and stakeholders, including hospital staff, clinical engineering staff and managers, maintenance personnel, procurement, and purchasing personnel.
* Travel will be regular within the defined region, and travel expenses will be covered according to Stryker's standard travel policy.
* Attend regular meetings and team meetings.
* Attend team meetings and give presentations to encourage team development.
* This is a field\-based position, with 80% travel. The remainder of the time will be home\-based/working remotely.

**What We Offer:**

* **We grow talent.** At Stryker we offer great personal and internal career development for our employees, with the possibility to make both vertical and lateral position movement.
* **Team spirit \& engagement**. We create engaged and inclusive teams that encourage colleagues to share their diverse perspectives and opinions.
* **Competitive salary**. With us, you will receive a competitive salary package and benefits such as: Company van, phone \& laptop and full technical equipment
* **Recognition of your performance**. At our company, outstanding individual and cross\-functional team performance is rewarded. Ideas, projects, or other exceptional achievements can be submitted for various awards on a local and global level.
* **Promoting work\-life balance through flexible working hours**. We offer an appealing and flexible working hours model to accommodate individual life situations.

**Required**

* Bachelor's degree in electronic/mechanical/biomedical engineering, equivalent study in medical device technology, or at least one of the following: Electrical / Electronics / Telecommunications / Mechanical / Software Technological subjects
* Fluent Italian \& English \-Spoken and written fluency (technical and medical):
* Adequate computer skills (Microsoft Office, Outlook).
* Minimum 1\-3 years of experience in medical device repair or in the hospital and healthcare industry, combined with good mechanical skills.
* IT experience at an administrative and technical level.
* Ability to build relationships with internal and external customers to foster brand loyalty.
* Service orientation, persistence, and a positive mindset.
* Stress tolerance and confidence in your work.
* Self\-discipline and work organization.

**Preferred:**

* Knowledge of Oracle, SAP, or SalesForce is a plus.


At Stryker, we believe transparency helps candidates make confident decisions about their next career step. Compensation is an important part of that process, and we want to be clear about how pay is determined. The compensation information posted for this role may be presented as a salary range or a fixed amount depending on the role. In addition to base salary, this role may be eligible for bonus, along with Stryker’s comprehensive benefits package.  

If you’d like to learn more about the salary or the benefits associated with this role, your recruiter will be happy to walk you through the details as you progress through the hiring process.

**Italy Pay Ranges:** €35\.700 \- €59\.400 EUR Annual


Stryker is a global leader in medical technologies and, together with its customers, is driven to make healthcare better. The company offers innovative products and services in MedSurg, Neurotechnology, Orthopaedics and Spine that help improve patient and healthcare outcomes. Alongside its customers around the world, Stryker impacts more than 150 million patients annually.
company_description: Stryker is a global leader in medical technologies and, together with our customers, we are driven to make healthcare better.

title: Junior Platform & IT Operations Engineer
description:
**About Us**
------------



The physical and digital worlds are merging. Vehicles, medical devices, industrial systems, critical infrastructure: security hasn't kept pace. AI is accelerating both the capabilities of the systems we depend on and the sophistication of the threats targeting them. A software vulnerability can now have physical consequences. A physical breach can compromise entire digital ecosystems.



Exein builds the security layer for this new reality. From kernel\-level runtime protection on embedded devices to cloud\-native platforms that analyze, protect, and prove compliance across entire device fleets. We're not patching the old model. We're building security from the core out.


**Job Overview**
----------------



We are looking for a motivated and proactive Junior Platform \& IT Operations Engineer to join our international Infrastructure team. This role is ideal for someone at the beginning of their career who wants to develop expertise in IT infrastructure, systems administration, networking, and platform operations.  

The successful candidate will initially support day\-to\-day IT operations, end\-user support, workstation provisioning, and infrastructure maintenance while progressively gaining exposure to enterprise platforms, cloud technologies, automation, and global infrastructure projects.  

Working in an international environment, the candidate will collaborate with colleagues across different countries and contribute to ensuring the reliability, security, and efficiency of the company's IT services and infrastructure.


**Responsibilities**
--------------------


* Provide first and second\-level IT support to employees, troubleshooting hardware, software, and connectivity issues.
* Configure, deploy, and maintain laptops, desktops, mobile devices, and peripherals.
* Manage user accounts, access permissions, onboarding, and offboarding activities.
* Support and maintain corporate network infrastructure, including LAN, Wi\-Fi, VPN, and related services.
* Assist with the administration and monitoring of servers, systems, and cloud environments.
* Perform routine maintenance activities, including patching, updates, backups, and infrastructure monitoring.
* Contribute to infrastructure documentation, operational procedures, and knowledge sharing.
* Collaborate with senior engineers on infrastructure, automation, and continuous improvement projects.

**Requirements**
----------------


* Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or a related field.
* Up to 3 years of experience in IT Support, Help Desk, System Administration, or Infrastructure roles.
* Knowledge of Linux environments, macOS, and endpoint management.
* Understanding of networking fundamentals.
* Familiarity with servers, virtualization, cloud platforms, or IT infrastructure concepts.
* Good communication skills and professional proficiency in English, both written and spoken.

  


**Why Join Us**


* Join a fast\-growing international scale\-up with ambitious global expansion plans.
* Be part of a young, collaborative, and multicultural team where your ideas and contributions matter.
* Develop your skills through continuous learning, mentoring, and hands\-on experience with modern technologies.
* Benefit from career growth opportunities and internal mobility as the company continues to scale.
* Work in a dynamic environment where innovation, ownership, and professional development are strongly encouraged.

  


**Location:** Rome \- Hybrid, Piazzale Flaminio 19


**Salary:** €30K– €40K

  


*Exein is committed to creating an inclusive workplace. We evaluate all applications fairly regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, background, or orientation.*

title: Farmacista collaboratore
description:
Farma Acquisition Srl, unità operativa Maraini, seleziona un/una Collaboratore/Collaboratrice Farmacista da inserire a tempo pieno nella sede di Rieti. Siamo una realtà con più punti vendita, orientata alla professionalità, al servizio al cliente e allo sviluppo delle risorse interne. L’ambiente di lavoro è dinamico, veloce e fortemente orientato al servizio al paziente. Orario e turnazione **Orario preferenziale: 08:00–20:00\.**  Turnazione: turni misti (mattina, pomeriggio e spezzati). **Turni ASL notturni previsti; disponibilità per notturni e festivi richiesta, con limitazioni concordabili. Requisiti**  Laurea in Farmacia o CTF e iscrizione all’Ordine dei Farmacisti. **Esperienza comprovata minima: 3–5 anni in farmacia.**  Conoscenze approfondite in farmacovigilanza; preferibile esperienza in farmacoterapia pediatrica. **Ottime capacità relazionali e comunicative.**  Attitudine al lavoro di squadra, responsabilità e problem solving. **Disponibilità a lavorare su turni, compresi notturni/festivi se necessario. Mansioni principali**  Assistenza al banco e consulenza al cliente su farmaci e parafarmaci. **Gestione ordini, scorte e controllo scadenze.**  Segnalazione e gestione di eventi avversi (farmacovigilanza). **Attività amministrative di supporto alla farmacia.**  Collaborazione con il team per garantire un servizio efficiente e orientato al paziente. Offerta **Contratto: CCNL Farmacie Private, tempo indeterminato (dettagli retributivi e inquadramento definiti in sede di colloquio).**  Retribuzione indicativa: €30\.000–€35\.000 lordi annui. **Orario: full‑time.**  Ambiente di lavoro: farmacia di quartiere in posizione centrale a Rieti, dinamico e orientato al servizio. **Opportunità di crescita professionale all’interno di una struttura con più farmacie. Trattamento dati personali I dati forniti saranno trattati conformemente alla normativa vigente sulla protezione dei dati personali. Contatti per candidatura Inviare curriculum vitae aggiornato e lettera di presentazione a: elleesseservizi@libero.it — oggetto: “Candidatura Collaboratore/a Farmacia Maraini — Rieti”. Per informazioni: 0746802895\.**

Retribuzione: €30\.000,00 \- €35\.000,00 all'anno

Benefit:

* Buoni pasto
* Parcheggio libero

Sede di lavoro: Di persona

title: Farmacista collaboratore/collaboratrice - Rieti
description:
Unisciti al nostro team e contribuisci al benessere delle persone!
  
  

Chi siamo
  
  

Farma Acquisition Holding, parte del The Boots Group, è un Retailer Farmaceutico che gestisce oltre 90 Farmacie di proprietà presenti in più di 10 regioni italiane.
  
  

Le nostre radici affondano negli anni '70 con Alleanza Farmaceutica, storica realtà della Distribuzione all'ingrosso di farmaci. Da questa esperienza è nato il nostro percorso nella gestione diretta delle Farmacie, con l'obiettivo di offrire un servizio sempre più vicino alle persone e ai bisogni di salute delle comunità locali.
  
  

Nel 2018 Boots è arrivata in Italia, portando con sé una storia iniziata nel 1849 nel Regno Unito e un'esperienza riconosciuta a livello internazionale nel campo della salute, del benessere, dell'innovazione e della qualità del servizio.
  
  

In Boots crediamo che la Farmacia sia molto più di un luogo dove dispensare farmaci: è uno spazio di ascolto, prevenzione e consulenza, in cui ogni professionista può contribuire concretamente al benessere delle persone e fare la differenza ogni giorno.
  
  

Per le nostre Farmacie di Rieti siamo alla ricerca di un/una Farmacista che desideri crescere in un ambiente dinamico, innovativo e orientato alla qualità del servizio.
  
  

Principali attività
  
  

La risorsa sarà inserita all’interno della Farmacia e contribuirà allo svolgimento delle attività professionali, operative e gestionali necessarie al corretto funzionamento del punto vendita.
  
  

Si occuperà della dispensazione di farmaci, parafarmaci e prodotti per la salute, della consulenza qualificata al cliente/paziente e della vendita assistita di dispositivi medici, integratori, prodotti dermocosmetici, articoli sanitari e prodotti per il benessere.
  
  

Parteciperà inoltre all’erogazione dei Servizi di Farmacia e della “Farmacia dei Servizi”, inclusi Servizi di screening, prevenzione, autoanalisi, prenotazioni, telemedicina e altre prestazioni attivabili secondo la normativa vigente.
  
  

La risorsa supporterà il Team nelle attività di gestione del magazzino, riordino, controllo delle scorte, verifica delle scadenze, corretta conservazione dei prodotti, utilizzo dei gestionali aziendali e gestione degli adempimenti amministrativi e documentali.
  
  

Collaborerà, inoltre, al rispetto della normativa di settore e delle procedure relative ai rapporti con ASL/ATS ed enti competenti, alla gestione dei farmaci stupefacenti, alla tracciabilità, alla farmacovigilanza e agli ulteriori adempimenti previsti per l’esercizio dell’attività farmaceutica.
  
  

Completano le attività la collaborazione con il Team di Farmacia, il rispetto delle procedure aziendali e il contributo al mantenimento di elevati standard di qualità del servizio, efficienza gestionale e continuità operativa.
  
  

Competenze e capacità richieste
  
  

Cerchiamo una persona con solida preparazione professionale, orientamento alla qualità del servizio e attenzione alla relazione con il cliente/paziente.
  
  

Sono richieste capacità di ascolto, comunicazione e consulenza, unite a precisione, affidabilità e senso di responsabilità nello svolgimento delle attività quotidiane.
  
  

Completano il profilo spirito di collaborazione, flessibilità organizzativa, attitudine al lavoro in team e interesse a contribuire all’evoluzione della farmacia come presidio sanitario di prossimità, nel rispetto degli standard professionali, aziendali e normativi.
  
  

Cosa offriamo
  
CCNL Farmacie Private
  
Contratto a tempo indeterminato
  
Formazione continua in ambito scientifico, consulenziale e gestionale
  
Sistema incentivante legato ai risultati
  
Percorsi di crescita professionale all'interno del Gruppo
  
Ambiente collaborativo e orientato al benessere delle persone
  
Work\-life balance garantito
  
  

Retribuzione e benefit
  
RAL indicativa tra € 30\.000 ed € 36\.000, commisurata all'esperienza e alle competenze
  
Buoni pasto
  
Sistema premiante annuale
  
  

Percorsi di sviluppo professionale e manageriale
  
  

Fai la differenza nella vita delle persone unendo la tua professionalità alla nostra missione!
  
Se sei un/una Farmacista appassionato/a, motivato/a e desideroso/a di lavorare in un contesto dinamico e innovativo, non esitare a candidarti.

title: Director, Quality Operation
description:
**Work Schedule**


Flex Shifts 40 hrs/wk**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**

**Director, Quality**
=====================

**Pharma Services Group (PSG) – Drug Product Division**
-------------------------------------------------------

### **Location: Ferentino, Italy site**


At Thermo Fisher Scientific, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life every day to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner, and safer.


We provide our teams with the resources, leadership, and opportunities needed to achieve their career goals while helping to take science a step beyond. From accelerating drug development to supporting life\-changing therapies, our work directly impacts patients worldwide.

**The Opportunity**
-------------------


We are seeking an experienced and strategic **Director of Quality** to join our Pharma Services Group (PSG) Drug Product Division at our Ferentino manufacturing site.


As a key member of the Site Leadership Team, you will provide leadership and direction for Quality Operations, Compliance \& Batch Release activities. You will ensure the highest standards of product quality, regulatory compliance, and inspection readiness while supporting the continued growth of one of Thermo Fisher Scientific’s most important pharmaceutical manufacturing sites.


This role offers the opportunity to partner closely with customers, regulatory authorities, corporate quality leadership, and operational teams to drive a culture of quality excellence, continuous improvement, and operational performance.

**Key Responsibilities**
------------------------

### **Quality Leadership \& Compliance**

* Provide strategic leadership for Quality Operations, Compliance \& Batch Release,
* Ensure ongoing compliance with cGMP, FDA, EMA, MHRA, and other applicable regulatory requirements.
* Maintain a state of inspection readiness and successfully lead regulatory inspections and customer audits.
* Act as the primary quality representative for critical compliance matters and quality\-related business decisions.

### **Quality Operations \& Product Release**

* Ensure timely disposition and release of products through effective management of quality systems and batch certification processes.
* Provide oversight of investigations, deviations, CAPAs, change controls, complaints, and risk management activities.
* Support Qualified Person (QP) activities and ensure robust product lifecycle quality oversight.
* Drive effective quality review and escalation processes to proactively identify and mitigate risk.

### **Customer \& Regulatory Engagement**

* Serve as a trusted quality partner to customers, regulatory agencies, and corporate stakeholders.
* Lead customer quality governance meetings and manage quality agreements.
* Support regulatory submissions, inspections, and responses to regulatory observations.
* Provide strategic guidance during customer onboarding, technology transfers, and commercial product launches.

### **Continuous Improvement \& Operational Excellence**

* Drive continuous improvement initiatives across manufacturing, laboratory, and supply chain operations.
* Utilize quality metrics and performance indicators to identify trends and opportunities for improvement.
* Champion quality culture, digital transformation initiatives, and operational excellence programs.
* Promote proactive risk management and data\-driven decision making throughout the organization.

### **Leadership \& Talent Development**

* Lead, coach, and develop high\-performing quality teams.
* Build organizational capability through succession planning, talent development, and employee engagement.
* Foster a culture of accountability, collaboration, and continuous learning.
* Partner effectively across Operations, Technical Services, Supply Chain, Engineering, and Corporate Quality functions.

**Minimum Qualifications**
--------------------------

* Advanced Degree with 6\+ years of experience, or Bachelor’s Degree with 8\+ years of experience in a regulated pharmaceutical, medical device, biologics, diagnostics, or related industry.
* Degree in Life Sciences, Engineering, Chemistry, Pharmacy, or related scientific discipline.
* Minimum 5 years of leadership experience within Quality organizations.
* Strong working knowledge of cGMP requirements, FDA regulations, EU GMP guidelines, and global quality standards.
* Proven experience leading regulatory inspections and customer audits.
* Demonstrated expertise in Quality Systems, CAPA, investigations, change control, risk management, and batch disposition.
* Experience managing customer relationships and quality agreements.
* Strong leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
* Demonstrated ability to lead organizational change and continuous improvement initiatives.
* Project management, financial planning, and resource management experience.

**Preferred Qualifications**
----------------------------

* Qualified Person (QP) certification or eligibility preferred.
* Professional certifications such as ASQ, RAPS, or equivalent.
* Experience within CDMO and contract manufacturing environments.
* Experience supporting global customer portfolios and complex supply chains.
* Knowledge of digital quality systems and quality management software platforms.
* Experience working within matrix and multi\-site organizations.

**Leadership Competencies**
---------------------------

* Sets strategic direction and translates vision into execution.
* Builds and develops high\-performing teams.
* Creates a culture of quality, compliance, and accountability.
* Influences effectively across functions, customers, and global stakeholders.
* Drives innovation, operational excellence, and continuous improvement.
* Demonstrates strong business acumen and decision\-making capability.

**Travel**
----------

* Up to 30% domestic and international travel may be required.

title: Creative Producer
description:
**CapsLock** is a dynamic marketing company providing IT\-enabled solutions for large North American partners. We specialize in scalable customer acquisition through data\-driven marketing, creative production, and technology innovation.


Our teams collaborate across marketing, IT, analytics, and media buying to build, test, and optimize high\-performing funnels that help clients achieve predictable growth across industries such as Home Improvement, Health, Legal, and Insurance.

  

We’re looking for a **Creative Producer** who thinks like a researcher \- someone who senses trends, understands storytelling, and knows how to turn marketing insights into videos that actually sell.


Your role is not only production, but to lead creative evolution \- generating ideas, refining proven creative approaches, finding new styles, testing them, and scaling what works. You’ll work at the intersection of project management, creative, analytics, and production.


You’ll also shape and pitch creative concepts that challenge existing ad formats and explore new storytelling angles.

 **Responsibilities:**

* Monitor and document emerging video trends across Meta, TikTok, YouTube, and other platforms.
* Market and Content development Research
* Research new video styles, not just iterate on existing ones.
* Translate insights into creative hypotheses.
* Project management, supporting various teams from idea to delivery
* Ensure each concept accurately reflects the client’s value proposition and target audience.
* Coordinate creators, influencers, and bloggers.
* Develop and manage test plans with media buyers to validate creative hypotheses.
* Review performance data (CTR, hook rate, watch time, CPA).
Understand a consistent “create test learn* scale” workflow for ongoing creative optimization.

Communicating effectively between marketing, creation and testing departments.  
* 

  

**AI \& Experimentation Mindset:**


You know how to experiment with AI tools \- from generating references in Midjourney to using Claude to organise your workspace. We don’t expect you to know every tool, but we value a “prompt\-first” mindset \- when you create faster than you search.

  

Requirements:  

  

**Essential**

* 3\+ years of experience in marketing \- ideally within paid social or performance marketing.
* Solid understanding of short\-form video formats and what drives performance on Meta, TikTok, and YouTube.
* Comfortable collaborating with creators and bloggers: sourcing, briefing, and overseeing remote shoots.
* Confident in interpreting marketing performance data.
* Exceptional communication and organization skills \- able to keep projects on track, people aligned, and expectations clear.
* Strong attention to detail, accountability, and the ability to prioritize multiple projects effectively.

Fluent English \- both spoken and written  
* 

  

**Nice to Have**

* Experience in media buying or working closely with media buyers.
* Familiarity with AI tools for generating or enhancing creative assets.
* Video production or video marketing background.
* Experience using UGC or influencer platforms to source and manage creators.
* Understanding of A/B testing and how creative experiments are structured and analyzed.

 **Why do we deserve your attention?**


Not only do we offer fair and competitive pay, but we also provide perks and benefits that support your well\-being and growth. Here are some of them:

 **Remote Work**


We are a truly remote\-first company. You choose where you feel the most productive and comfortable to make an impact.

 **Paid Time Off**


Work\-life balance matters to us, and we support it with paid time off for rest, personal time, or simply doing what makes you happy.

 **Ongoing Learning**


We believe in curiosity and growth. That’s why we support continuous learning with workshops, online courses, conferences, and certifications.

 **Home Office**


We help you set up your perfect workspace by covering equipment and furniture expenses, so you can do your best work from home.

 **Physical Well\-Being**


From gym memberships and yoga classes to home workout equipment or even a bicycle, we give you the flexibility to stay active your way.

 **Unlimited Sick Days**


Your health matters. Take the time you need to rest and recover \- we’ll be here when you’re ready to return.

 **Medical Coverage**


We support you and your family with reimbursement for medical insurance and health\-related expenses, including dental care and psychological support.

 **Co\-Working Space**


If you sometimes crave a change of scenery or more social interaction, we’ll cover the full cost of a co\-working space.

 **Fun Stuff**


From gaming nights and fitness challenges to annual retreats at world\-class resorts, we make sure there’s never a shortage of fun and connection.

title: Mid-Level Instructional Designer (Contract, Remote)
description:
**OUR HIRING PROCESS:**


* We will review your application against our job requirements. We do not employ machine learning technologies during this phase as we believe every human deserves attention from another human. We do not think machines can evaluate your application quite like our seasoned recruiting professionals—every person is unique. We promise to give your candidacy a fair and detailed assessment.
* We may then invite you to submit a video interview for the review of the hiring manager. This video interview is often followed by a test or short project that allows us to determine whether you will be a good fit for the team.
* At this point, we will invite you to interview with our hiring manager and/or the interview team. Please note: We do not conduct interviews via text message, Telegram, etc. and we never hire anyone into our organization without having met you face\-to\-face (or via Zoom). You will be invited to come to a live meeting or Zoom, where you will meet our INFUSE team.
* From there on, it's decision time! If you are still excited to join INFUSE and we like you as much, we will have a conversation about your offer. We do not make offers without giving you the opportunity to speak with us live.  

  

INFUSE is committed to complying with applicable data privacy and security laws and regulations. For more information, please see our Privacy Policy
 **INFUSE** is a demand generation company providing solutions to B2B organizations. We help clients and partners to deliver audience, buyers, and account engagement that meets their goals. With a global team operating across more than 60 countries, the company has been recognized multiple times on the prestigious Inc. 5000 list, and has received over 60 other industry awards, including Inc. Best Workplaces.  

  

We are seeking a creative and strategic **Instructional Designer** to craft impactful learning experiences that combine the best of digital and human interaction. In this role, you will partner with subject matter experts and stakeholders to design modern, engaging programs for adult professionals—leveraging both evidence\-based instructional design principles and the latest in generative AI technologies.



**INFUSE Academy** drives both internal and external online education, creating smart, high\-impact e\-learning experiences that fuel real business results.



We're not just checking boxes – we're designing innovative, engaging, and practical e\-learning programs that empower people to grow and thrive.



As we scale, we're looking for a strategic and growth\-oriented Instructional Designer to join our fast\-paced, tight\-knit team of passionate professionals. If you're someone who brings bold ideas, loves experimenting with new approaches, and thrives in a collaborative environment, this is your moment.



### **Our L\&D philosophy**



We're committed to delivering practical, growth\-driven education that aligns with both business objectives and learner needs. Most of our solutions are designed as self\-paced, asynchronous e\-learning experiences, enhanced with AI\-powered tools and immersive learning simulators.



We believe learning should be engaging and story\-driven. Inspired by platforms like Netflix, we continuously experiment with storytelling techniques to make development experiences more compelling and impactful.

  

**We're looking for someone who:**


* Has a background in **instructional design** and **AI implementation**
* Understands the capabilities and limitations of **LLMs (like GPT\-4\)**
* Can prototype and configure **custom GPTs and similar AI tools** for learning solutions
* Is **detail\-oriented, innovative, and comfortable** in a fast\-paced, iterative environment
* Loves solving problems **creatively** and making learning experiences **smarter** and more **engaging**

### **Key responsibilities**


* Collaborate with internal subject matter experts (SMEs) and key stakeholders to design and deliver effective blended learning programs that align with business goals
* Apply principles of andragogy and evidence\-based instructional design to create engaging, learner\-centric experiences for adult professionals
* Design and curate blended learning solutions using a mix of learning formats (e.g., eLearning, self\-paced modules, virtual training sessions, and on\-the\-job activities)
* Ensure instructional consistency, accessibility, and relevance of content across delivery modes
* Leverage generative AI tools, including designing and deploying custom GPTs, to support learning
* Manage project timelines, resources, and communication workflows to ensure the timely and organized execution of learning initiatives

### **What we offer**


* A dynamic and supportive team environment focused on innovation in learning.
* Meaningful involvement in training initiatives with global impact.
* Career growth potential within a fast\-paced, purpose\-driven team.
* Access to cutting\-edge e\-learning tools and approaches.
* Access to high\-quality professional development resources and marketing insights.


If you recognize yourself in this and are up to the challenge, we'd be happy to hear from you!



We are an Equal Opportunity Employer. We make hiring decisions based on qualifications, merit, and business needs. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, sexual orientation, and gender identity), national origin, age, disability, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected under applicable law. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and believe that diversity of experience, culture, and perspective makes us stronger.
